question_text: A book is resting on a table and is not moving. What can you say about the forces acting on the book?

options.label: A
options.text: There are no forces acting on the book at all

options.label: B
options.text: The forces acting on the book are balanced

options.label: C
options.text: The downward force is much larger than the upward force

options.label: D
options.text: There is only a downward force acting on the book

explanation: When an object is stationary (not moving), the forces acting on it must be balanced. This means the upward support force from the table exactly equals the downward weight of the book, so there is no net force and the book stays at rest.
